claim: decision-makers modulate attention to others' preferences based on both dispositional self-focus and the scale of the affected group.
uncertain self-conception drives retention of low-use ongoing purchases by making discontinuation feel like identity loss.
perceiving choice options as unified wholes rather than attribute bundles shifts comparison from feature-by-feature to global evaluation.
quantified elements facilitate downstream ellipsis resolution during parsing by engaging a unified compositional mechanism.
documented political misperceptions reflect partisan cheerleading more than genuine false belief.
subjective probability of obtaining outcomes shapes the anchoring point against which gains and losses are evaluated.
individuals just above a disadvantage threshold resist closing the gap because redistribution threatens their marginal advantage.
stable personality dimensions correlate with distributed variation in cortical thickness and surface properties.
early scholastic setbacks scar long-term educational attainment primarily through middle-stage expectations that can be revised early in secondary schooling.
older workers' preference for emotionally meaningful tasks emerges from shortened time horizons and is eliminated when horizons are experimentally manipulated.
intensive early behavioral intervention produces measurable gains in cognitive and adaptive functioning when delivered in community settings.
a weak preceding stimulus can inhibit the pain response to an intense stimulus, with greatest effect for pain-sensitive individuals.
the size of declared online social networks maps onto localized variation in brain regions supporting social perception and memory.
humans value coordinated action intrinsically and choose to collaborate even when solo performance is more efficient.
individual affective traits and some personality dimensions predict one's own relationship satisfaction more than one's partner's traits.
spirituality encompasses multiple independent dimensions (existential, cognitive, experiential, paranormal, religious) distinct from standard personality traits.
the nonlinearity of probability weighting intensifies near certainty/impossibility for emotionally vivid outcomes but not for affectively muted ones.
attitudes toward novel neutral objects develop through implicit detection of pairings with valenced stimuli, without explicit memory for the pairings.
